# t0lkim.dev — Full Content

> Mike Lott — Cybersecurity & Operational Engineering

## About Mike Lott

I build things.

---

## Project: TeenTidal

**What is TeenTidal?** TeenTidal is a native iOS app that wraps Tidal's SDK with two-layer explicit content filtering. It provides structural parental controls with no user-bypassable settings.

**What problem does TeenTidal solve?** Tidal's built-in explicit content toggle can be re-enabled by any user from within the app. iOS Screen Time's "Clean" restriction only applies to Apple Music, not third-party apps. Parents have no reliable way to give children safe access to Tidal.

**How does TeenTidal work?** TeenTidal uses two independent filtering layers: metadata-level filtering (checking Tidal's explicit flag on tracks, albums, and playlists) and a secondary content analysis layer. Both must pass before content plays. There are no user-facing settings to disable filtering — the controls are structural, not configurable.

**Language:** Swift | **Platform:** iOS | **Status:** Alpha, pending Tidal developer programme access
---

## Project: pg-harden

**What is pg-harden?** pg-harden is a Rust CLI tool for PostgreSQL security hardening. It scans, reports, and enforces a deny-all security posture across database instances.

**What problem does pg-harden solve?** PostgreSQL ships with permissive defaults — local trust authentication, MD5 password hashing, unencrypted connections, and unrestricted public schema access. Hardening guides require manual, repetitive work across every instance. There is no practical way to verify consistent security baselines across a fleet.

**How does pg-harden work?** pg-harden connects to PostgreSQL instances and checks SCRAM authentication enforcement, SSL/TLS configuration, pg_hba rules, public schema access, and other security settings against best practices. It produces structured reports and can enforce remediation.

**Language:** Rust | **Category:** Security Tools
---

## Project: TidalPlaylistBuilder

**What is TidalPlaylistBuilder?** TidalPlaylistBuilder is a Rust CLI that builds Tidal playlists from BBC programmes, CSV files, and curated text track listings. It supports multi-volume artist anthologies.

**What problem does TidalPlaylistBuilder solve?** Tidal has no way to import playlists from external sources. Building comprehensive artist anthologies (hundreds of tracks spanning decades) manually is impractical — each track must be searched and added individually.

**How does TidalPlaylistBuilder work?** It authenticates via OAuth device flow, parses track listings from multiple input formats (BBC programme pages, CSV files, plain text), fuzzy-matches tracks against Tidal's catalogue, and creates or updates playlists via the Tidal API. The anthology system organises large collections into numbered volumes.

**Language:** Rust | **Authentication:** OAuth 2.0 device flow
---

## Project: TheStreamDeck

**What is TheStreamDeck?** TheStreamDeck is a Raspberry Pi-based unified streaming audio appliance with a touchscreen display, physical analogue VU meters, and instant source switching across Tidal, Plexamp, BBC radio, and internet radio.

**What problem does TheStreamDeck solve?** An audiophile with multiple streaming subscriptions experiences frustrating source switching — each service runs on a different device, requiring manual amp input selection. The fragmented workflow disrupts listening sessions.

**How does TheStreamDeck work?** One Raspberry Pi drives all four audio sources through a single amp input. A touchscreen displays album artwork and controls. Physical VU meters show real-time audio levels. Source switching is instant — tap a button on the touchscreen to change from Tidal to BBC radio without touching the amplifier.

**Platform:** Raspberry Pi | **Sources:** Tidal HiFi Plus, Plexamp, BBC 6 Music, internet radio
---

## Project: PatronVault

**What is PatronVault?** PatronVault is a Rust CLI for downloading and archiving media content from Patreon creators you subscribe to.

**What problem does PatronVault solve?** Patreon has no export feature for patrons. If a creator deletes a post, changes tiers, or leaves the platform, paid content is lost. The official API is creator-focused with no subscriber-facing download endpoints.

**How does PatronVault work?** PatronVault authenticates via browser cookies against Patreon's internal JSON:API, enumerates posts and media attachments, and downloads everything with metadata preservation.

**Language:** Rust | **Authentication:** Cookie-based
---

## Project: CourseVault

**What is CourseVault?** CourseVault is a Go CLI for downloading and archiving course content from Teachable platforms.

**What problem does CourseVault solve?** Online courses on Teachable have no built-in download or export feature. If your subscription lapses or the course is removed, you lose access to material you paid for.

**How does CourseVault work?** CourseVault extracts browser cookies for authentication, enumerates the full course curriculum, and downloads every video at original quality with metadata embedding, preserving the course structure.

**Language:** Go | **Authentication:** Browser cookie extraction
---

## Project: ThreeM

**What is MMM?** MMM (MyMediaManager) is a Rust CLI for organising images and videos with deduplication, EXIF-based renaming, and date-based directory structure.

**What problem does MMM solve?** Photos and videos accumulate across phones, cameras, SD cards, and cloud downloads. The same image exists in multiple places under different names. Camera-assigned filenames carry no meaningful information. Manual sorting is impractical at scale.

**How does MMM work?** MMM reads EXIF metadata from media files, renames them with date-based conventions, deduplicates by content hash, and organises into a clean date-based directory structure.

**Language:** Rust | **Category:** Media Organisation
---

## Project: session-manager

**What is Session Manager?** Session Manager is an LLM-agnostic orchestrator that coordinates concurrent AI sessions. It detects entity-tag overlap between sessions, provides conflict warnings when two sessions modify the same files, and injects relevance-filtered context so each session knows what related sessions are doing — without broadcasting everything to everyone.

**What problem does Session Manager solve?** LLM sessions are isolated. Each starts with zero knowledge of other active sessions. The operator manually relays context and resolves conflicts. With multiple concurrent sessions, context loss, blind file conflicts, and lack of fleet visibility become significant problems.

**How does Session Manager work?** Sessions are launched via a thin shim that captures PID and writes heartbeats. The orchestrator (a Rust binary) monitors heartbeats, infers entity tags from session behaviour (file I/O, SSH connections, git activity), computes overlap, and writes per-session context files with only relevant information. ~70% of orchestration is deterministic compiled code with zero LLM tokens. Only narration synthesis uses an LLM (Haiku), at ~6,000 tokens/hour.

**Language:** Rust | **Category:** Developer Tools
---

## Project: FretNote

**What is FretNote?** FretNote is an iPadOS-primary guitar note-learning app that announces a target note and octave, listens to what the user plays via microphone, and judges accuracy using YIN autocorrelation pitch detection with cents-level precision. Gamified session loop with post-session analytics.

**What problem does FretNote solve?** Most guitarists learn patterns (chord shapes, scale boxes) without knowing which note their finger is on. No existing tool drills note identification across the full fretboard with real-time feedback from a live guitar.

**How does FretNote work?** The app announces "A, octave three" via speech synthesis. The user plays the note on their guitar. A YIN pitch detector (pure Swift, zero dependencies) identifies the played pitch and a judge determines the verdict: Clean (±10 cents), Correct (±25 cents), Close (±50 cents), or Wrong. Post-session analytics break down accuracy by note name, fretboard region, and octave band.

**Language:** Swift 6.0 | **Platform:** iPadOS 17+ / iOS 17+ | **Distribution:** Commercial
---

## Project: ATV-deJunker

**What is ATV-deJunker?** A bash script that debloats Android TV devices by replacing the stock Google TV launcher with AT4K, removing preloaded bloatware, installing streaming apps, and disabling sponsored content.

**What problem does ATV-deJunker solve?** Android TV devices ship with bloatware, recommendation ads, and a stock launcher that promotes sponsored content. There is no built-in way to remove these. ATV-deJunker automates the entire cleanup via ADB over WiFi.

**How does ATV-deJunker work?** Connect to the TV via ADB, install streaming apps (Netflix, Prime Video, Apple TV, YouTube, Plex, Disney+), install AT4K launcher, disable the stock launcher, remove bloatware packages, disable home panel ads, and reboot into a clean experience. Recovery is one command if anything goes wrong.

**Language:** Bash | **Category:** System Tools | **Tested on:** Prism+ Android TV 10
---
